meta.name: PCS Pain Catastrophizing Scale
meta.description: The PCS is a 13-item self-report scale that assesses catastrophic thinking related to pain experiences. It includes three dimensions: Helplessness, Magnification, and Rumination, and is widely used in pain management and clinical research.
meta.author: Sullivan, Bishop & Pivik (1995)
meta.reference: Sullivan MJL, Bishop SR, Pivik J. The Pain Catastrophizing Scale: development and validation. Psychol Assess. 1995;7(4):524-532.
meta.instruction: When you are in pain, you may have various thoughts and feelings. Please rate how often you experience each of these when in pain.

questions.text: I worry all the time about whether the pain will end
I feel I can’t go on
It’s terrible and I think it’s never going to get any better
It’s awful and I feel that it overwhelms me
I feel I can’t stand it anymore
I become afraid that the pain will get worse
I keep thinking of other painful events
I anxiously want the pain to go away
I can’t seem to keep it out of my mind
I keep thinking about how much it hurts
I keep thinking about how badly I want the pain to stop
There’s nothing I can do to reduce the intensity of the pain
I wonder whether something serious may happen

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing level is low. You cope well with pain experiences.
suggestions: Continue maintaining positive pain coping strategies
Seek appropriate pain management when needed

ranges.label: Mild Catastrophizing
ranges.description: You have some degree of pain catastrophizing and may occasionally exaggerate the threat of pain.
suggestions: Learn pain management and coping skills
Consult a pain management specialist if needed

ranges.label: Moderate Catastrophizing
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing level is elevated. This may increase the risk of chronic pain and affect recovery.
suggestions: Consider seeking professional pain management help
Cognitive-behavioral therapy can effectively reduce catastrophizing
Learn to distinguish pain sensations from thoughts about pain
Discuss comprehensive pain management with your healthcare team

ranges.label: Severe Catastrophizing
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing level is very high. This may significantly affect your pain experience and daily functioning.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek help from a pain psychologist
Comprehensive pain management (medication + psychological + physical therapy) may be most effective
Avoid excessive focus and rumination about pain

helplessness.label: High
helplessness.description: High helplessness. Consider learning active coping strategies.

helplessness.label: Very High
helplessness.description: Very high helplessness. Consider seeking professional help.
